Module com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java
Package com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.internal.extension

Class AbstractPrimitiveNumberVerifierNoOp<S,T extends Number & Comparable<? super T>>

java.lang.Object
com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.internal.extension.AbstractObjectVerifierNoOp<S,T>
com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.internal.extension.AbstractComparableVerifierNoOp<S,T>
com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.internal.extension.AbstractNumberVerifierNoOp<S,T>
com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.internal.extension.AbstractPrimitiveNumberVerifierNoOp<S,T>
Type Parameters:
S - the type of verifier returned by the methods
T - the type of the value being verified
All Implemented Interfaces:
ExtensibleComparableVerifier<S,T>, ExtensibleNumberVerifier<S,T>, ExtensibleObjectVerifier<S,T>, ExtensiblePrimitiveVerifier<S,T>
Direct Known Subclasses:
BigDecimalPrecisionVerifierNoOp, PrimitiveNumberVerifierNoOp, SizeVerifierNoOp
public abstract class AbstractPrimitiveNumberVerifierNoOp<S,T extends Number & Comparable<? super T>> extends AbstractNumberVerifierNoOp<S,T> implements ExtensiblePrimitiveVerifier<S,T>
An ExtensiblePrimitiveVerifier that does nothing. A validator that ignores all subsequent failures because they are guaranteed to fail and would add any value to the end-user. For example, an attempt was made to dereference null or cast the value to an incompatible type.

  • Method Details

    • isNegative

      @Deprecated public S isNegative()
      Deprecated.
      Description copied from interface: ExtensibleNumberVerifier
      Ensures that the actual value is negative. -0.0 is considered to be equal to zero.
      Specified by:
      isNegative in interface ExtensibleNumberVerifier<S,T extends Number & Comparable<? super T>>
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
